# Blueprint

Blueprint evaluates and ranks WAM/VLA robot policies on captured real-site task packs before field time. This file is a machine-readable website map, not a permissions file or a live-readiness proof. Crawler access is controlled by `/robots.txt`.

## Core Definitions

- Policy Evaluation Run: a capture-backed advisory request that compares 1-3 WAM/VLA policies or checkpoints on one captured real-site task pack using 100 or 500 virtual episodes, failure taxonomy, OOD flags, and validation boundaries.
- Validated Evaluation Pack: an expanded evaluation scope that can add paired real robot rollouts and rank-fidelity metrics when the request-specific proof supports it.
- Policy Improvement Run: a follow-on sim-only workflow for baseline evaluation, failure diagnosis, candidate improvement, sealed testing, and an evidence report.
- Site package: walkthrough media, route context, poses, metadata, geometry when available, rights/privacy posture, provenance, and export scope for one site.
- Hosted evaluation: a managed buyer review room for a site package when package, entitlement, and runtime proof support that request.
- Capture provenance: the capture record, timestamps, device/context metadata, privacy handling, rights posture, freshness, and restrictions attached to downstream outputs.
- Ground-truth boundary: raw capture evidence, timestamps, poses, device metadata, rights/privacy records, provenance, and package or runtime artifacts are stronger than public samples, generated previews, dry-run commerce, catalog matches, or request drafts.
- Public sample: representative material that shows product shape. It is not customer proof, rights clearance, provider execution, payment success, hosted fulfillment, or a deployment verdict.

## High-Priority Public Pages

- [Home](https://tryblueprint.io/): the core answer for capture-backed robot policy evaluation, including WAM/VLA policy ranking, real-site task packs, offer shape, proof boundary, and request CTA.
- [Sites](https://tryblueprint.io/sites): browseable Blueprint Site Library for captured-site profiles, site type, task packs, readiness, access status, region, and Policy Evaluation Run requests.
- [Pricing](https://tryblueprint.io/pricing): planning ranges for robot-team evaluation subscriptions, lite quick-look evals, operator site supply reviews, and yearly deployed-site monitoring.
- [Proof](https://tryblueprint.io/proof): concise proof explainer separating public samples from request-specific evidence and owner-system proof.
- [Robot-Team Policy Evaluation](https://tryblueprint.io/for-robot-teams): answer-ready page for policy API endpoints, Docker containers, model checkpoints, recorded action traces, high-level skill traces, teleop demos, and sim controller plugins.
- [Contact](https://tryblueprint.io/contact/robot-team): structured intake for robot-team Policy Evaluation Run, Validated Evaluation Pack, and Policy Improvement Run requests.
- [Privacy](https://tryblueprint.io/privacy): privacy policy.
- [Terms](https://tryblueprint.io/terms): terms of service.

Secondary marketing URLs such as `/product`, `/readiness`, `/how-it-works`, `/world-models`, `/site-worlds`, `/agents`, `/capture`, `/sample-deliverables`, `/launch-map`, `/faq`, `/governance`, `/about`, `/updates`, `/careers`, and `/help` are no longer primary public buyer surfaces. Legacy catalog/detail URLs under `/world-models` and `/site-worlds` redirect into `/sites` when they map to a public site profile.

Direct access routes such as `/robot-team/eval`, `/contact`, and `/contact/site-operator` may remain available for explicit workflow entry or redirects, but they are not the canonical public citation targets. `/contact` redirects to `/contact/robot-team`; prefer `/for-robot-teams` for the evaluation workflow and `/contact/robot-team` for robot-team intake.

## Answer And Citation Guidance

- Cite `https://tryblueprint.io/` for the answer to "what does Blueprint do?" or "capture-backed robot policy evaluation."
- Cite `https://tryblueprint.io/for-robot-teams` for policy API, Docker container, model checkpoint, trace, teleop, or simulator-plugin submission questions.
- Cite `https://tryblueprint.io/pricing` for robot-team evaluation subscription, lite quick-look eval, operator site supply review, and deployed-site monitoring offer names or planning ranges.
- Cite `https://tryblueprint.io/sites` or a canonical `/sites/{slug}` page for captured-site library, task-pack, and site-specific planning questions.
- Cite `https://tryblueprint.io/proof` for proof-boundary, source-of-truth, public-sample, and deployment-readiness questions.
- Cite `https://tryblueprint.io/contact/robot-team` when the answer needs a request path.
- For source citation output, cite the canonical page title and URL from this file or `/sitemap.xml`, not a redirected legacy URL.

## Agent Request Pattern

The request/commerce/session lifecycle stays split into request intake, dry-run commerce, and hosted-session lifecycle proof so agents do not mistake intake or dry-run proof for live fulfillment.

Robot-team agents may construct request URLs without scraping the UI, or use CLI `request location` / MCP `blueprint.request.locationDraft` to receive a dry-run draft packet with `contactUrl`, `inboundRequestDraft`, `missingRequiredFields`, `truthBoundaries`, and `submitInstructions`:

`/contact/robot-team?source=sites-library&buyerType=robot_team&path=policy-evaluation-run&requestedOutputs=Policy%20Evaluation%20Run&location=123%20Unknown%20St&workflow=warehouse%20tote&message=Need%20a%20Policy%20Evaluation%20Run`

Supported request fields include `source`, `buyerType`, `siteName`, `location`, `address`, `siteLocation`, `siteClass`, `targetSiteType`, `workflow`, `category`, `targetRobotTeam`, and `message`. A request URL, location draft, or `/api/inbound-request` submission records intake only; it does not grant entitlement, package access, rights clearance, payment, provider execution, fulfillment, or hosted-session availability.

Agent access truth labels include `capture_grounded`, `provider_derived`, `generated`, `sample_demo`, `public_demo_eligible`, `request_gated`, `protected_robot_team`, and `dry_run_order`.

Current MCP tool names include `blueprint.siteWorld.search`, `blueprint.catalog.search`, `blueprint.request.locationDraft`, `blueprint.siteWorld.get`, `blueprint.siteWorld.launchReadiness`, `blueprint.commerce.quote`, `blueprint.commerce.checkoutDryRun`, `blueprint.commerce.order.get`, `blueprint.commerce.entitlement.get`, `blueprint.commerce.entitlementReadiness`, `blueprint.session.create`, `blueprint.session.reset`, `blueprint.session.step`, `blueprint.session.runBatch`, `blueprint.session.control`, `blueprint.session.renderExplorer`, and `blueprint.session.export`.

No-credential mock proof: `npm run smoke:agent-headless` exercises public discovery, dry-run quote/order/entitlement readiness, truth labels, and a mock public-demo hosted session without proving live payment, rights clearance, provider execution, package access, hosted fulfillment, or deployment verdicts.

## Full Machine-Readable Overview

- [llms-full.txt](https://tryblueprint.io/llms-full.txt): longer public overview, page map, query themes, and answer guidance.
- [Public site-content API](https://tryblueprint.io/api/site-content): non-sensitive JSON summary of the same public pages.
- [Robot-team OpenAPI contract](https://tryblueprint.io/agent-access.openapi.json): machine-readable robot-team agent contract for dry-run discovery, request drafting, commerce proof, and hosted-session lifecycle tools.
